title: LooselyBaked Uruguayan Pillowlamb Bake
description: A traditionally-inspired, ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly dish from Uruguay that combines the bitter flavors of red pepper and the pillowy texture of lamb, all in one pot.
instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the chopped red pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
